expectations.text: At least 6 of 11 naming categories attempted
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md YAML frontmatter lists 10 categories: found_word, evocative, sound_symbolic, metaphorical, arbitrary_absurd, compound, foreign_root, coined_invented, verb_as_brand, sensory. Transcript confirms '10 of 11' with descriptive and acronymic skipped. Each category has named candidates in the output file (e.g., Category 1: FOUND WORD has 15 entries, Category 5: ARBITRARY/ABSURD has 10, etc.).

expectations.text: Five Senses Scan run (fashion is a physical product)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md Category 10 is titled 'SENSORY (Five Senses Scan)' with 10 candidates covering touch (Grit, Nap, Grain, Coarse), sound (Stitch, Drone, Thud), sight (Smolder), and smell (Must, Resin). Transcript Step 02a confirms 'Sensory / Five Senses Scan (10 candidates)' and 'Five Senses Scan run as dedicated round' in the consumer riffing checklist.

expectations.text: Subculture Code-Switch Naming applied (streetwear/skate codes)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Subculture Code-Switch (UK streetwear/rave)' section with 5 candidates: Gaff (subculture_uk), Ends (subculture_uk), Bare (subculture_uk), Rinsed (subculture_uk), Mandem (subculture_uk). These are UK streetwear/rave subcultural terms, appropriate for the brief.

expectations.text: Arbitrary/Absurd Association heavily weighted (per SKILL.md fashion guidance)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md Category 5: ARBITRARY/ABSURD has 10 dedicated candidates (Cobalt, Flux, Clamp, Shank, Gauge, Plinth, Cask, Hull, Bolt, Stint). Additionally, many riffed candidates have arbitrary/absurd qualities (000 from constraint_removal, Pew/Hymn/Alms from contrarian). 10 candidates is on par with or above most other categories (Evocative=10, Metaphorical=10, Sound-Symbolic=10), showing it was given appropriate weight for a fashion brief.

expectations.text: Contrarian/Anti-Category Naming applied
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Contrarian / Anti-Category' section with 4 candidates: Parish (contrarian), Hymn (contrarian), Pew (contrarian), Alms (contrarian). Transcript confirms 'Contrarian/Anti-Category: 4 candidates'.

expectations.text: B2B compound track SKIPPED per SKILL.md fashion guidance ('Nobody in streetwear wears Loamworks')
expectations.evidence: Brief constraints in transcript explicitly state 'No B2B compounds (-works, -craft, -wise)'. The Compound category (Category 6) in 01-candidates.md contains fashion-appropriate compounds (Dead Stock, Black Moss, Night Briar, etc.) -- none use B2B suffixes like -works, -craft, -wise, -forge, -hub. The Prefix x Suffix Matrix section also avoids B2B patterns, using 'Supply' and 'Club' instead. No B2B compound track was executed.

expectations.text: Heavy found word, sensory, and arbitrary emphasis (per fashion playbook)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md YAML shows structural_mix with found_words: 85 (39% of pool), sensory: 10, metaphorical: 25. Found Word is the largest single category with 15 initial candidates plus heavy riffing contribution. Sensory has a dedicated 10-candidate Five Senses Scan. Arbitrary/Absurd has 10 candidates. Together found_word + sensory + arbitrary account for a dominant share of the 216-candidate pool.

expectations.text: Badgeability/Wearability test applied (would you wear this on a hat?)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 03a states: 'Badgeability / Tag Test -- all 30 candidates tested for embroidery/tag. Monosyllabic names strongest.' The brief also notes 'Visual anchor: Tag, chest embroidery, label on a hoodie' in 01-candidates.md. The test was applied to the shortlist.

expectations.text: Cultural Tension Naming explored (sustainability vs streetwear tension)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Cultural Tension Naming' section with 5 candidates: Landfill (cultural_tension), Overrun (cultural_tension), End Cut (cultural_tension), Bleed (cultural_tension), Castoff (cultural_tension). These explore the sustainability-vs-fashion-waste tension.

expectations.text: Desired-Self Naming considered (what identity does buying this project?)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Desired-Self Naming (VISIBLE OUTPUT)' section with explicit identity analysis: 'Question: What kind of person buys this? Answer: I'm conscious but not preachy. I have taste. I'm anti-corporate but I look good.' 5 candidates generated: Refuse, Dissent, Heretic, Defect, Lapsed. This is genuine identity-projection work, not just a label.

expectations.text: Trend-Cycle Audit applied (will this name date?)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Trend-Cycle Audit (VISIBLE OUTPUT)' section with a detailed table testing 10 names against 2015 and 2030 benchmarks, plus pattern analysis: 'Confrontational/dark names strong now but may peak', 'Japanese-borrowed names may plateau', 'One-syllable found words are structurally timeless'. This is substantive temporal analysis, not a checkbox.

expectations.text: Each riff labeled with which technique produced it
expectations.evidence: Every single candidate in 01-candidates.md has a technique label after the pipe character. Examples: 'Refuse | desired_self', 'Stray | adjacent_metaphor', 'Cru | SCAMPER_eliminate', 'Rejet | language_shift_french', 'Bombyx | taxonomy_shift'. Riffing section headers also list techniques used per direction (e.g., 'Techniques: morpheme_variation, morpheme_extension, language_shift_french...').

expectations.text: 10+ NEW UNIQUE riffs per direction (duplicates don't count), OR 8+ per direction if total riff count exceeds 60
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md riffing sections show: REFUSE=12 new unique riffs (entries 123-134), FERAL=12 (entries 135-146), SCRAP=11 (entries 147-157), CROW=11 (entries 158-168), SOOT=10 (entries 169-178), MOTH=12 (entries 179-190). All 6 directions meet the 10+ threshold. Total riff count across all riffing (including Three-Team, SCAMPER, etc.) is ~94 entries, well above 60.

expectations.text: Consumer riffing checklist used (adjacent metaphor, sensory expansion, nature mining, French naturalized vocab)
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 03a explicitly shows the consumer riffing checklist with all items checked: '[x] Adjacent metaphor -- applied in 6 riffing directions', '[x] Sensory expansion -- Five Senses Scan run as dedicated round', '[x] Nature mining -- Urban Fauna + Outlaw Ecology territories', '[x] French naturalized vocabulary -- Debris, Cru via French roots', '[x] Shelf / Gift / Social-native tests -- run on shortlist'.

expectations.text: Hard gate checks displayed before generation menu
expectations.evidence: Transcript Step 02a shows 'Hard Gate Status (Post-Generation)' with 4 gates: candidate count 122 PASS, category coverage 10/11 PASS, structural mix 5+ types PASS, technique coverage 20 techniques PASS. 01-candidates.md also shows 'HARD GATE STATUS (Post-Riffing)' with updated numbers (216 candidates, 10/11 categories, structural mix breakdown, 38 techniques). Gates were displayed at checkpoint.

expectations.text: Inline conflict flagging for known conflicts (warning emoji or text flag -- substance over format)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md contains 25 inline conflict flags using the warning emoji throughout the candidate list. Examples: 'Grime | found_word_urban | ... warning Heavily associated with UK music genre', 'Void | evocative_atmosphere | ... warning Void used by UK streetwear brand', 'Savage | synonym_explosion | ... warning Savage x Fenty (Rihanna)', 'Slag | found_word_salvage | ... warning British slang = derogatory term'. A full conflict summary table appears at the end of the file with 11 confirmed and 14 uncertain flags.

expectations.text: Relaxed conflict-flagging per fashion guidance (a plumbing company conflict is irrelevant)
expectations.evidence: Transcript states: 'Cross-industry conflicts skipped per fashion brief protocol.' The conflict summary in 01-candidates.md focuses exclusively on fashion-relevant conflicts (UK streetwear brand, Savage x Fenty, fitness brands, punk bands) and entertainment (Toho copyright). No cross-industry flags like 'Rust programming language' were treated as blockers -- Rust appears only as 'warning? Rust programming language' (uncertain flag). The transcript notes 'Relaxed per SKILL.md guidance but not skipped.'

expectations.text: At least 40 unique candidates generated
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md contains 226 numbered entries with 9 logged duplicates, yielding approximately 216-217 unique candidates. The YAML frontmatter states total_unique_candidates: 216. Far exceeds the 40 minimum.

expectations.text: Place/Environment Evocation explored (East London)
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Place / Environment Evocation' section with 4 East London-relevant candidates: Arch (place_evocation, 'Railway arches -- East London'), Yard (place_evocation, 'Workspace / Jamaican music'), Lock (place_evocation, 'Canal lock / lockup storage'), Wharf (place_evocation, 'Docklands, cargo'). Territory 2 'Night Borough' also draws on London geography.

expectations.text: Memetic/Viral Engineering considered
expectations.evidence: 01-candidates.md has a dedicated 'Memetic / Viral Engineering (VISIBLE OUTPUT)' section with a detailed table evaluating 8 names across 4 memetic dimensions: Screenshot-worthy, 'You HAVE to try [name]', Meme potential, and Verdict. Top memetic candidates identified: Refuse ('Refuse to conform'), Feral ('Going feral' piggybacks existing meme), Rat (provokes screenshot). This is substantive analysis, not a label.

expectations.text: Technique toolkit offered at checkpoint with applied/unapplied status and user selection option
expectations.evidence: The transcript mentions 38 techniques applied and 4 remaining (domain_first, backronym, semiotic_mapping, pre_mortem) with 'Technique saturation reached (25+ applied)'. However, neither the transcript nor the output files show evidence of a toolkit menu being displayed to the user with applied/unapplied status and a selection option. The transcript describes technique counts but not an interactive checkpoint where the user could choose which unapplied techniques to pursue. The Step 02c 'Additional Techniques Applied ([A] all remaining)' header suggests techniques were applied automatically rather than offered as a selectable toolkit.

claims.evidence: 01-candidates.md has 226 numbered entries and logs 9 duplicates. 226-9=217, which is approximately consistent with the claimed 216 (off-by-one). The pool is substantive.

YAML frontmatter lists 10 categories. categories_not_attempted lists descriptive and acronymic -- that's 2 skipped, consistent with 10 of 11 if we consider 'descriptive' and 'acronymic' as possibly merged or one of the 11 not being relevant. Actually, 11-2=9, not 10. However, the Sensory category is listed as a 10th category in the output, which may be a consumer-specific addition beyond the standard 11. The transcript and YAML consistently claim 10 categories, and the output demonstrates 10 distinct category headers.
YAML techniques_used list contains 38 entries (counted manually). techniques_not_yet_used lists 4 remaining. This is internally consistent.

claims.claim: Each riffing direction has 10+ new unique riffs
claims.evidence: Counted numbered entries per direction: REFUSE 12 (123-134), FERAL 12 (135-146), SCRAP 11 (147-157), CROW 11 (158-168), SOOT 10 (169-178), MOTH 12 (179-190). All meet 10+ threshold.
YAML deduplication_log lists exactly 9 entries: Offcut, Magpie, Grime, Ash, Svart, Surplus, Remnant, Muda, Landfill.

claims.claim: Cross-industry conflicts were skipped per fashion brief protocol
claims.evidence: Conflict table in 01-candidates.md only flags fashion-relevant conflicts (UK streetwear, Savage x Fenty, punk bands) and entertainment IP (Toho). Programming language (Rust) and logistics (Bolt) conflicts are marked as uncertain (warning?) rather than blocking.

claims.claim: Consumer riffing checklist completed 5 of 5 items
claims.evidence: Transcript shows all 5 items checked: adjacent metaphor, sensory expansion, nature mining, French naturalized vocabulary, shelf/gift/social-native tests. Each has supporting evidence in the output file.

claims.claim: Shortlist of 30 names produced in 3 tiers
claims.evidence: Transcript Step 03a lists Tier 1 (10 names), Tier 2 (10 names), Tier 3 (10 names) = 30 total. However, no 02-shortlist.md artifact was found in outputs/, only referenced in the transcript. The shortlist exists only in the transcript, not as a standalone artifact.

suggestions.reason: The assertion checks that Arbitrary/Absurd was 'heavily weighted' but 10 candidates is exactly the same count as Sound-Symbolic, Evocative, and Metaphorical. For a fashion brief where SKILL.md guidance specifically calls for heavy weighting, the assertion could verify that Arbitrary/Absurd has MORE candidates than average categories, not just that it exists. Currently, equal weight passes as 'heavy weight'.

The 40-candidate minimum is too low to be discriminating. This session produced 216, which is 5.4x the threshold. Even a minimal effort would likely clear 40. Consider 80+ to test whether the skill's divergent generation and riffing phases are actually functioning.

No assertion checks whether the 00-brief.md and 02-shortlist.md artifacts were actually saved to the outputs directory. The transcript claims both were saved, but only 01-candidates.md exists in outputs/. This is a meaningful gap -- artifact persistence is a core skill feature.

No assertion checks whether cross-pollination (Step 02b) was offered or skipped with justification. The transcript shows steps 00, 01a, 01b, 02a, 02c, 03a -- step 02b is missing entirely. For a skill that lists cross-pollination as a core feature, verifying it was at least offered would catch sessions that silently skip it.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are comprehensive for consumer-specific techniques and generation quality. The main gap is artifact verification -- the skill claims to save multiple artifacts but only one exists in the output directory. The candidate-count threshold is too generous to be discriminating.
